reason: Shared TypeScript types for the entire app: pipeline stages, jobs, annotations, sections, project config, and all API response/request shapes
description: Central type definitions used across frontend components, API routes, and server libraries. Defines: PipelineStage, StageStatus, StageStatusEntry, Job, JobStatus, Annotation, AnnotationAnalysis, AnnotationCaptureData, CreateAnnotationInput, FixType, Section, ProjectConfig, TtsConfig, VeoConfig, RenderConfig, TtsSegment, WordTimestamp, RenderProgress, SseSend, ClaudeFixResult, VeoClip, AssetStagingEntry, SpecSection, CompositionSection, SectionRenderStatus, FullVideoInfo, AuditSectionResult. All other modules import from here — no circular deps.

functions.returns: union type

functions.returns: union type

reason: package.json defines all project dependencies, scripts, and metadata for the Next.js app
description: Node.js package manifest. Key dependencies: next@15, react@19, react-dom@19, better-sqlite3, @google/generative-ai, @remotion/renderer, wavesurfer.js, codemirror, tailwindcss@4. DevDependencies: typescript, @types/node, @types/react, @types/better-sqlite3. Scripts: dev (next dev), build (next build), start (next start). Includes engines field requiring node>=20.

reason: tsconfig.json configures TypeScript compilation for Next.js App Router with strict mode
description: TypeScript configuration for Next.js 15. Key settings: target ES2017, lib [dom, dom.iterable, esnext], moduleResolution bundler, strict: true, paths alias @/* for root imports. Extends next/core-web-vitals preset. Include app/, lib/, components/, scripts/ directories.

reason: next.config.ts must configure serverExternalPackages for better-sqlite3 and ffmpeg bindings, plus SSE streaming timeouts
description: Next.js 15 App Router configuration. Key settings: serverExternalPackages: ['better-sqlite3'], experimental.serverActions.bodySizeLimit for large file uploads, custom headers for SSE cache-control, and any needed rewrites. Uses TypeScript config format (next.config.ts).

context_urls.purpose: serverExternalPackages config for native modules like better-sqlite3
Route handler config for SSE streaming

reason: SQLite database module is the persistence layer for jobs, annotations, and pipeline stage status
description: Server-only module wrapping better-sqlite3. Exports: getDb() singleton returning a Database instance, schema migration on first open (creates tables: jobs, annotations, pipeline_status). Uses synchronous API (required for Next.js Route Handlers). Handles crash recovery: on startup marks any 'running' job as error with message 'Server restarted during pipeline'.

context_urls.purpose: better-sqlite3 API reference for synchronous SQLite access

functions.name: getDb
functions.returns: better-sqlite3 Database singleton

functions.name: runMigrations
functions.returns: void — creates tables if missing

functions.name: recoverCrashedJobs
functions.returns: void — marks running→error on startup

reason: Project config module reads, writes, and hot-reloads project.json — the single source of truth for all pipeline state
description: Server-only module managing project.json. Exports: loadProject() reads and validates project.json with Zod. saveProject(config) writes atomically (write to .tmp then rename). watchProject(callback) uses fs.watch for hot-reload. getSection(id) utility. validateProjectConfig(data) returns typed ProjectConfig or throws ZodError. Used by every API route that needs section registry or TTS/Veo config.

functions.name: loadProject
functions.returns: validated ProjectConfig

functions.name: saveProject
functions.signature: function saveProject(config: ProjectConfig, dir?: string): void
functions.returns: void — atomic write

functions.name: getSection
functions.signature: function getSection(id: string, config: ProjectConfig): Section | undefined
functions.returns: Section or undefined

reason: SSE utility module standardizes Server-Sent Events streaming across all API routes
description: Server-only module providing SSE streaming helpers for Next.js Route Handlers. Exports: createSseStream() returns { stream: ReadableStream, send, done, error } — the send() function writes 'data: ...\n\n' events, done() sends event:done, error() sends event:error. Handles encoder/controller lifecycle. Used by all pipeline API routes that return streaming responses.

context_urls.purpose: SSE protocol: event format, reconnection, event types
Next.js ReadableStream pattern for SSE in Route Handlers

functions.name: createSseStream
functions.signature: function createSseStream(): { stream: ReadableStream; send(data: object): void; done(): void; error(msg: string): void }
functions.returns: SSE stream + control handles

reason: Claude CLI integration module spawns claude subprocess for analysis (read-only) and fix (read-write) invocations
description: Server-only module managing Claude Code CLI invocations. Two exported functions: runClaudeAnalysis(prompt, files) spawns 'claude -p --model claude-opus-4-6 --output-format json --allowedTools Read,Glob --no-session-persistence' and returns parsed JSON result. runClaudeFix(prompt, files, scopeDir) spawns with Read,Write,Edit,Glob,Grep tools scoped to scopeDir. Both functions stream stderr to a log callback, parse JSON output with three fallback strategies (direct parse, code fence extraction, brace matching), and return typed results. Hard timeout: 300s.

functions.name: runClaudeAnalysis
functions.signature: function runClaudeAnalysis(prompt: string, onLog?: (line: string) => void): Promise<AnnotationAnalysis>
functions.returns: parsed AnnotationAnalysis JSON

functions.name: runClaudeFix
functions.signature: function runClaudeFix(prompt: string, scopeDir: string, onLog?: (line: string) => void): Promise<ClaudeFixResult>
functions.returns: { fixType, filesModified, changeDescription, confidence }

reason: Veo/Imagen API client module handles video and reference image generation via Google GenAI SDK
description: Server-only module wrapping google-genai SDK for Veo and Imagen. Exports: generateReferenceImage(prompt, outputPath) calls imagen-3.0-generate-002 and saves PNG. generateVeoClip(prompt, referenceImagePath, aspectRatio, outputPath) calls veo-3.1-generate-preview, polls operation until complete, downloads and saves MP4. extractLastFrame(clipPath, outputPath) runs ffprobe + ffmpeg subprocess to extract last frame for frame chaining. All functions throw on API error with descriptive message.

functions.name: generateReferenceImage
functions.signature: function generateReferenceImage(prompt: string, outputPath: string): Promise<void>
functions.returns: void — writes PNG to outputPath

functions.name: generateVeoClip
functions.signature: function generateVeoClip(prompt: string, referenceImagePath: string | null, aspectRatio: '16:9' | '9:16', outputPath: string): Promise<void>
functions.returns: void — writes MP4 to outputPath

functions.name: extractLastFrame
functions.signature: function extractLastFrame(clipPath: string, outputPath: string): Promise<void>
functions.returns: void — writes PNG last frame via ffmpeg

reason: Render module wraps Remotion renderer API and ffmpeg for section rendering and full-video stitching
description: Server-only module for Remotion rendering and ffmpeg stitching. Exports: renderSection(compositionId, outputPath, onProgress) uses @remotion/renderer renderMedia() — NOT CLI subprocess. stitchFullVideo(sectionPaths, outputPath, onProgress) runs ffmpeg -f concat -safe 0. getSectionDuration(mp4Path) runs ffprobe. renderStill(compositionId, frame, outputPath) for audit screenshots. All progress callbacks receive {percent: number, message: string}.

context_urls.purpose: Remotion renderMedia() API for programmatic rendering without CLI
Remotion renderStill() for audit frame captures

functions.name: renderSection
functions.signature: function renderSection(compositionId: string, outputPath: string, onProgress: (p: RenderProgress) => void): Promise<void>
functions.returns: void — renders section MP4

functions.name: stitchFullVideo
functions.signature: function stitchFullVideo(sectionPaths: string[], outputPath: string, onProgress: (p: RenderProgress) => void): Promise<void>
functions.returns: void — ffmpeg concat

functions.name: getSectionDuration
functions.returns: duration in seconds via ffprobe

functions.name: renderStill
functions.signature: function renderStill(compositionId: string, frame: number, outputPath: string): Promise<void>
functions.returns: void — renders single PNG frame

reason: Global CSS file for Tailwind v4 CSS-first configuration with @theme{} custom design tokens
description: Tailwind v4 global stylesheet. Uses @import 'tailwindcss' syntax (no tailwind.config.js). Defines @theme {} block with custom color tokens matching the video editor UI: sidebar dark background, stage status badge colors (done=green, running=amber, error=red, not_started=gray), annotation severity colors. Also defines @layer base with body font and dark background defaults.

context_urls.purpose: Tailwind v4 CSS-first config: @import, @theme{}, no tailwind.config.js
Tailwind @theme{} token definitions for custom colors and spacing

reason: Job manager module is the critical orchestrator for pipeline stage execution, queueing, progress tracking, and retry logic
description: Server-only module managing job lifecycle. Exports: createJob(stage, params) inserts into DB, returns jobId. runJob(jobId, executor) marks running, calls executor(onLog), marks done/error. getJob(jobId) reads from DB. retryJob(jobId) resets status and re-runs. cancelJob(jobId). runPipelineStage(stage, params, onEvent) handles prerequisite auto-run: checks dependency graph, runs incomplete upstream stages concurrently before requested stage. Dependency graph is defined here as a DAG: audio track (tts-script→tts-render→audio-sync) runs in parallel with visual track (specs→veo), then compositions, then render, then audit.

functions.name: createJob
functions.signature: function createJob(stage: PipelineStage, params: Record<string, unknown>): string
functions.returns: jobId string

functions.name: runJob
functions.signature: function runJob(jobId: string, executor: (onLog: (msg: string) => void) => Promise<void>): Promise<void>
functions.returns: void — marks done or error

functions.name: getJob
functions.returns: Job record from DB

functions.name: retryJob
functions.returns: void — resets and re-runs same executor

functions.name: runPipelineStage
functions.signature: function runPipelineStage(stage: PipelineStage, params: Record<string, unknown>, send: SseSend): Promise<string>
functions.returns: jobId — auto-runs prerequisites concurrently

reason: render_tts.py is the Qwen3-TTS subprocess spawned by the TTS render API to synthesize audio segments
description: Python script invoked by the Node.js TTS render API route as a child process. Reads narrative/tts_script.md, parses [TONE:], [PACE:], [PAUSE:], [EMOTION:] annotation tags, generates one WAV file per NARRATOR: segment using Qwen3-TTS model. Accepts CLI args: --segments (comma-separated segment IDs, default: all), --output-dir (default: outputs/tts/). Prints JSON progress lines to stdout: {segmentId, status, duration}. Exits 0 on full success, non-zero if any segment failed.

functions.name: main
functions.signature: def main(segments: list[str] | None, output_dir: str) -> None
functions.returns: None — writes WAV files, prints JSON progress to stdout

functions.name: render_segment
functions.signature: def render_segment(segment_id: str, text: str, tone: str, pace: str, output_dir: str) -> dict
functions.returns: dict with segmentId, status, duration

reason: sync_audio_pipeline.py concatenates TTS segments into per-section WAV files, copies to remotion/public, and generates word-level timestamps
description: Python script invoked by the audio sync API route as a child process. Reads project.json sectionGroups to determine which segments map to which section. For each section: concatenates segment WAVs in order (using pydub or ffmpeg), copies result to remotion/public/{section}/narration.wav, runs faster-whisper with word timestamps, writes word_timestamps.json to outputs/tts/{section}/. Prints JSON progress per section. Args: --project-dir (default: .), --output-dir (default: outputs/tts/), --remotion-public (default: remotion/public/).

functions.name: main
functions.signature: def main(project_dir: str, output_dir: str, remotion_public: str) -> None
functions.returns: None — writes WAV + timestamps, prints JSON progress

functions.name: concatenate_section
functions.signature: def concatenate_section(section_id: str, segment_paths: list[str], output_path: str) -> None
functions.returns: None — ffmpeg concat

functions.name: generate_timestamps
functions.signature: def generate_timestamps(wav_path: str, output_json: str) -> None
functions.returns: None — writes word_timestamps.json via faster-whisper

reason: generate_section_compositions.py scaffolds Remotion section wrapper components from project.json section registry
description: Python script invoked by the compositions API route to generate boilerplate Remotion section wrapper components. For each section in project.json, creates remotion/src/remotion/{sectionId}/index.tsx with a Sequence wrapper, imports each sub-composition, and sets up timing from offsetSeconds/durationSeconds. Also updates remotion/src/remotion/Root.tsx to register all sections as top-level Compositions. Args: --project-dir, --remotion-dir (default: remotion/). Prints JSON progress per section. Skips sections where the file already exists (unless --force).

functions.name: main
functions.signature: def main(project_dir: str, remotion_dir: str, force: bool) -> None
functions.returns: None — generates TSX files, prints JSON progress

functions.name: generate_section_wrapper
functions.signature: def generate_section_wrapper(section: dict, remotion_dir: str, force: bool) -> None
functions.returns: None — writes {sectionId}/index.tsx

functions.name: update_root
functions.signature: def update_root(sections: list[dict], remotion_dir: str) -> None
functions.returns: None — rewrites Root.tsx registrations

reason: Project API route handles reading and updating project.json, including section registry and config
description: Next.js API route for project configuration. GET /api/project returns the full ProjectConfig JSON. PUT /api/project accepts partial ProjectConfig updates, validates with Zod, writes atomically to project.json, returns updated config. Returns 400 on validation error with Zod issues. Returns 500 on file write failure. Used by Stage 1 Project Setup panel.

reason: Script API route manages reading and writing narrative/main_script.md for the Stage 2 editor
description: Next.js API route for script file access. GET /api/project/script reads narrative/main_script.md and returns { content: string }. PUT /api/project/script accepts { content: string }, writes to disk atomically. Returns 404 if file doesn't exist. The auto-save debounce (1s) is handled client-side; this route is called on each debounced write.

reason: Pipeline status API provides a poll endpoint for refreshing all 10 stage badges without page reload
description: Next.js API route returning current status of all 10 pipeline stages. GET /api/pipeline/status reads pipeline_status table from DB and returns { stages: Record<PipelineStage, { status: StageStatus, lastJobId: string | null, error: string | null }> }. Polled every 5s by the client sidebar. No auth.

reason: TTS script generation API triggers Claude Code to extract narration and add TTS annotations from main_script.md
description: POST /api/pipeline/tts-script/run triggers a Claude Code invocation that reads narrative/main_script.md and writes narrative/tts_script.md with [TONE:], [PACE:], [PAUSE:], [EMOTION:] annotations. Returns { jobId }. SSE stream available at /api/jobs/:id/stream. Uses lib_jobs runPipelineStage which auto-runs Stage 1+2 prerequisites. Claude is invoked with Read+Write tools, scoped to narrative/.

reason: TTS render API triggers Qwen3-TTS rendering of all or specified segments from tts_script.md
description: POST /api/pipeline/tts-render/run accepts { segments?: string[] } — if omitted, renders all. Spawns scripts/render_tts.py as a child process, streams stdout/stderr via SSE. Returns { jobId }. Each segment produces outputs/tts/{segmentId}.wav. GET /api/pipeline/tts-render/segments returns list of all segments parsed from tts_script.md with current WAV status.

reason: Audio sync API triggers sync_audio_pipeline.py to concatenate segments, copy to remotion/public, and generate word timestamps
description: POST /api/pipeline/audio-sync/run triggers scripts/sync_audio_pipeline.py. Reads sectionGroups from project.json to determine segment-to-section mapping. Returns { jobId }. SSE streams per-section progress. Output: per-section narration WAVs in remotion/public/, word_timestamps.json per section in outputs/tts/{section}/. GET /api/pipeline/audio-sync/timestamps?section=X returns the timestamps JSON for the word viewer.

reason: Spec generation API triggers Claude Code to expand visual descriptions into detailed spec markdown files per section
description: POST /api/pipeline/specs/run accepts { sections?: string[], files?: string[] }. If sections omitted, generates all. Invokes Claude Code with Read+Write tools scoped to specs/. Returns { jobId }. GET /api/pipeline/specs/list returns tree of specs by section with visual type badges (Remotion/veo/title/split). GET /api/pipeline/specs/file?path=X returns spec file content for the inline editor. PUT /api/pipeline/specs/file saves spec file content.

reason: Veo generation API orchestrates character reference image creation, clip generation, frame chaining, and split-screen compositing
description: POST /api/pipeline/veo/run accepts { clips?: string[] } — generates specified clips or all. Uses lib_veo for Imagen and Veo API calls, implements frame chaining by extracting last frame of clip N before generating clip N+1. Returns { jobId }. GET /api/pipeline/veo/clips returns clip list with section, aspect ratio, status, frameChain deps. POST /api/pipeline/veo/references/run regenerates a character reference portrait. GET /api/pipeline/veo/staging-manifest returns expected vs present remotion/public/ files.

context_urls.purpose: Veo generateVideos API with image reference and frame chaining

reason: Composition generation API triggers Claude Code to write Remotion components from specs and generates section wrapper scaffolding
description: POST /api/pipeline/compositions/run accepts { components?: string[], wrappers?: string[] }. Invokes Claude Code with Read+Write+Edit+Glob tools scoped to remotion/src/remotion/ for each component. Also runs scripts/generate_section_compositions.py for section wrappers. Returns { jobId }. POST /api/pipeline/asset-staging/run copies files from outputs/veo/ to remotion/public/ per staging manifest. GET /api/pipeline/compositions/list returns components and wrappers by section with status.

context_urls.purpose: Remotion Composition registration and props schema with Zod

reason: Render API triggers Remotion section rendering and ffmpeg full-video stitching with parallel render support
description: POST /api/pipeline/render/run accepts { sections?: string[] } — renders up to 3 in parallel per project.json render.maxParallelRenders. Uses lib_render renderSection(). Returns { jobId }. POST /api/pipeline/stitch/run runs ffmpeg concat using lib_render stitchFullVideo(). After render, reads ffprobe duration for each section and updates project.json durationSeconds + offsetSeconds. GET /api/pipeline/render/status returns per-section render status and full video info.

reason: Audit API launches parallel Claude Code agents that render Remotion stills and compare against specs
description: POST /api/pipeline/audit/run accepts { sections?: string[] } — defaults to all. Launches one agent per section concurrently, each calling lib_render renderStill() for segment midpoints then lib_claude runClaudeAnalysis() comparing frame vs spec. Writes AUDIT_*.md files. Returns { jobId }. GET /api/pipeline/audit/results returns per-section pass/fail counts and spec-level verdicts. The [Create Annotation →] action is handled client-side by pre-filling the annotation form.

reason: Jobs SSE stream API provides real-time progress streaming for job execution via Server-Sent Events
description: GET /api/jobs/[id]/stream opens an SSE stream (ReadableStream) — subscribes to job log events from DB and sends as SSE until job reaches done/error state. The SSE stream sends: { type: 'log', message }, { type: 'progress', percent }, then event: done or event: error. Used by SseLogPanel component.

endpoints.responseSchema: SSE stream: log | progress | done | error events

reason: Job polling API provides REST endpoint for querying current job state without SSE
description: GET /api/jobs/[id] returns the current Job record from DB for polling fallback when SSE is unavailable. Returns the full Job shape including status, progress, error, timestamps. Returns 404 if job not found.

reason: Job retry API re-runs a failed job by resetting its status and re-executing the same pipeline stage
description: POST /api/jobs/[id]/retry re-runs a failed job via lib_jobs retryJob(). Resets the job status from 'error' to 'running', re-executes the same executor function that was used originally, and returns the updated jobId. Returns 404 if job not found, 400 if job is not in error state.

reason: Annotations CRUD API handles creating and listing review annotations for sections
description: POST /api/annotations creates annotation with drawing/text/video fields, saves composite thumbnail (canvas+frame), returns created Annotation. GET /api/annotations?section=X returns all annotations for a section ordered by timestamp.

reason: Annotation by ID API fetches a single annotation record for detail view and editing
description: GET /api/annotations/[id] returns a single Annotation record from DB by ID. Returns the full Annotation shape including analysis results if analyzed. Returns 404 if annotation not found.

reason: Annotation analyze API triggers Claude read-only analysis of a specific annotation using spec files and screenshot
description: POST /api/annotations/[id]/analyze triggers Claude read-only analysis — spawns lib_claude runClaudeAnalysis() with the annotation's screenshot, the section's spec files, and the section's Remotion composition. Updates annotation.analysis field in DB with severity, fixType, technicalAssessment, suggestedFixes. Returns updated Annotation with analysis populated.

reason: Section resolve-batch API batches all pending annotations for a section, applies Claude fixes, and re-renders
description: POST /api/sections/[id]/resolve-batch gathers all unresolved annotations for the section, groups by fixType (remotion/veo/tts), and applies batch fixes: Remotion fixes use lib_claude runClaudeFix() scoped to remotion/src/, Veo fixes trigger veo regeneration, TTS fixes trigger re-synthesis. After all fixes, re-renders affected sections and stitches final video. Returns { jobId } for SSE progress tracking.

reason: Video streaming API serves MP4 files with HTTP range request support for the VideoPlayer component
description: GET /api/video/[...path] serves video files from outputs/sections/ and remotion/public/ with proper range request handling (206 Partial Content). Reads file size, parses Range header, returns appropriate slice. Sets Content-Type: video/mp4 and Accept-Ranges: bytes. Used by VideoPlayer <video> element. Restricts path traversal: only allows files within the project video directories.

reason: SSE log panel component streams real-time job output to users across all pipeline stages
description: Client component displaying a scrollable, auto-tailing log panel fed by EventSource. Props: jobId (string | null), onDone, onError. Opens EventSource to /api/jobs/{jobId}/stream when jobId is set. Appends [timestamp] message lines. Shows green ✓ banner on 'done' event, red ✕ on 'error'. Falls back to polling /api/jobs/{jobId} if EventSource fails. Auto-scrolls to bottom. Virtualized list for large logs. Renders null when jobId is null.

reason: Stage sidebar is the left-column navigation showing all 10 pipeline stages with status badges
description: Client component rendering the narrow left sidebar with 10 clickable stage rows. Each row shows: stage number, label, status badge (○ not_started, ◌ running, ● done, ✕ error). Active stage is highlighted. Polls /api/pipeline/status every 5s to refresh badges. Props: activeStage, onStageSelect(stage). Badges animate (pulse) when running. Error stages show tooltip with error message on hover.

reason: Video player component implements the spacebar annotation workflow with drawing canvas overlay and speech recognition
description: Client component implementing the core review UX. Internal canvas is 1920x1080 (scaled to fit container). Space bar toggles pause/record mode. While recording: freehand/rectangle/circle/arrow/text drawing tools active, Web Speech API transcription running. Second space capture: getImageData() for frame thumbnail, merge canvas+frame into composite via OffscreenCanvas, emit onAnnotationCapture. Also supports keyboard shortcuts: D=draw, M=mic, F/R/C/A/T=tool select, Ctrl+Z=undo, K=play/pause, arrow keys=seek. Displays annotation timestamps as marker dots on progress bar.

context_urls.purpose: Web Speech API SpeechRecognition for voice annotation capture
OffscreenCanvas for frame+drawing composite image creation

reason: Annotation panel component displays the list of review annotations with analysis status and batch resolve controls
description: Client component showing annotation cards for the current section. Each card shows: timestamp, drawing thumbnail, text, analysis severity badge (color-coded), fix type (remotion/veo/tts), analysis summary. Cards can be expanded to show full technicalAssessment and suggestedFixes. [Apply N Fixes] button triggers POST /api/sections/{id}/resolve-batch. Each resolving annotation shows inline progress (SSE via resolveJob.jobId). Failed annotations show [Retry] button. Completed annotations show green ✓.

reason: Stage 1 panel: Project Setup — form for initializing and editing project.json with section registry
description: Client component for Stage 1. Left: form fields for project name, outputResolution, TTS voice/rate, Veo model/aspectRatio, render config. Right: Section Registry table with inline-editable rows (click [✎] to edit). [+ Add Section] appends blank row with drag handles for reordering. [Save ✓] PUTs to /api/project. Unsaved changes show yellow dot and trigger beforeunload. Success shows toast.

reason: Stage 2 panel: Script Editor — split-pane CodeMirror editor with structured preview and TTS trigger
description: Client component for Stage 2. Left pane: CodeMirror 6 with Markdown syntax highlighting, auto-saves via PUT /api/project/script on 1s debounce. Right pane: structured preview re-rendering on 200ms debounce — NARRATOR: blocks get blue ■, [VISUAL:] blocks get teal ▣, ## headers get gray section labels. Resizable split via divider drag. [Generate TTS Script →] enabled only when ≥1 NARRATOR: block detected, POSTs to /api/pipeline/tts-script/run and advances to Stage 3.

context_urls.purpose: CodeMirror 6 API for markdown editor with syntax highlighting and controlled value

reason: Stage 3 panel: TTS Script Generation — SSE log, unified diff view, and inline editor
description: Client component for Stage 3. Header: [Generate TTS Script ↺] button + last run time. SSE log panel (SseLogPanel component). Below log: split diff view (source vs generated) with red/green/gray line coloring. Inline CodeMirror editor for tts_script.md (auto-saves on blur, 1s debounce). [Render Audio →] button enabled when tts_script.md exists. Generates diff client-side from both file contents.

reason: Stage 4 panel: TTS Rendering — segment list with per-row waveform playback and batch render controls
description: Client component for Stage 4. Toolbar: [Render All] and [Render Missing] trigger batch jobs. Segment table: columns #, segment ID, status badge, [▶] play, [↺] re-render. Row expansion shows WaveSurfer.js waveform for the WAV file plus raw TTS text. Batch progress bar with current segment ID at right. Uses WaveSurfer.js initialized on row expand. Per-row [↺] shows inline SseLogPanel for that row's job.

context_urls.purpose: WaveSurfer.js API for inline waveform rendering and audio playback

reason: Stage 5 panel: Audio Sync Pipeline — section grouping config editor, run controls, word timestamp viewer
description: Client component for Stage 5. Top: section grouping table (inline-editable segment ranges per section), [Save Config] PUTs to /api/project. [Run Audio Sync] POSTs to /api/pipeline/audio-sync/run, shows SseLogPanel. Bottom: Word Timestamp Viewer table (word, start, end, segmentId) with section filter dropdown and search. Virtual scrolling for large datasets (react-virtual or CSS contain). Timestamps in seconds with 3 decimal places.

reason: Stage 6 panel: Spec Generation — collapsible section groups with spec file list, visual type badges, and inline editor
description: Client component for Stage 6. [Generate All Specs] and [Regenerate Section] toolbar buttons. Section groups are collapsible (localStorage persisted). Each spec row shows visual type badge [Remotion]/[veo:]/[title:]/[split:], status, [✎] open in editor, [↺] regenerate. Inline CodeMirror Markdown editor auto-saves to spec file path via PUT /api/pipeline/specs/file. SSE log in expandable drawer. Per-section [↺] triggers section-scoped run.

reason: Stage 7 panel: Veo Generation — character references, frame chaining graph, clip list with stale warnings and auto-composite
description: Client component for Stage 7. Left column: character reference panel (thumbnail per portrait, [↺ Regenerate] per reference) + frame chaining dependency graph (text-based chain display). Right column: clip list table (clip ID, section, aspect ratio, status, stale ⚠ warning). [Generate All] / [Generate Missing] / dropdown for single section. Auto-composite checkbox for split: clip pairs. SSE progress shows per-clip progress. Stale warning badge if upstream clip was regenerated.

reason: Stage 8 panel: Composition Generation — component list with preview, section wrappers, and asset staging manifest
description: Client component for Stage 8. Left: component list by section (collapsible), each component has [Preview] (opens PNG still in modal) and [↺]. Section wrappers listed separately. [Generate All Compositions] runs batch. Right: asset staging manifest (expected vs present remotion/public/ files). ✓/✗ per file. [Stage Now] per missing file. [Stage All Missing] batch. Log drawer for per-component errors. Error status shows last error in tooltip.

reason: Stage 9 panel: Render + Stitch — parallel render progress bars, section table, and full video panel
description: Client component for Stage 9. Section render table: columns section, duration, status, progress bar (0-100%), [▶] modal preview, [↺] re-render. Active renders panel shows up to 3 simultaneous progress bars. [Render ▾] dropdown: All / Missing / Selected. [Stitch Full Video] POSTs to /api/pipeline/stitch/run. Full Video panel: file size, duration, [Open in Review →]. All progress bars update via SSE (jobId from each render call).

reason: Stage 10 panel: Audit — multi-agent audit results table with per-spec pass/fail verdicts and Create Annotation bridge
description: Client component for Stage 10. Section audit table: columns section, pass count, fail count, status, [View Report], [↺]. [Audit All Sections] / [Audit Section ▾] trigger jobs. [View Report] expands row to per-spec verdicts (pass/fail badge + one-line summary). FAIL rows show [View Frame] (opens still PNG in modal), [View Spec] (opens spec in inline viewer), [Create Annotation →] (pre-fills annotation form in Review tab with finding + frame). SSE shows per-agent progress.

reason: Root app layout provides the two-column shell (stage sidebar + main panel) and tab switching between Pipeline and Review modes
description: Next.js App Router root layout (app/layout.tsx). Sets up HTML structure: <html lang='en'>, dark body bg. Imports globals.css. Exports metadata. The actual two-column layout with Pipeline/Review tabs is in app/page.tsx (client component) since it manages tab state and stage selection.

reason: App page is the top-level client component managing tab state (Pipeline vs Review), active stage selection, and the two-column layout
description: Client component (app/page.tsx). Manages: activeTab ('pipeline' | 'review'), activeStage (PipelineStage). Two-column layout: left = StageSidebar (pipeline tab only) | right = active stage panel or review panel. Tab bar at top switches between Pipeline and Review. When activeTab='pipeline', renders the appropriate Stage1-10 component based on activeStage. When activeTab='review', renders VideoPlayer + AnnotationPanel side by side. Loads initial project config via GET /api/project on mount.

dataSources.description: Load initial project config on mount
